question.label: Team connection
question.name: How connected do you feel to the rest of the team right now?
question.description: Probes isolation or drift without naming it; one of the earliest signals of disengagement.

answer: Honestly kind of drained. I haven't been sleeping well and I'm behind on things.
note: They reported being drained and not sleeping well, with being behind on things pointing to a wellbeing hit and some disengagement risk.

question.label: Pacing tradeoff
question.name: What have you had to drop or slow down to keep up with everything on your plate?
question.description: Surfaces hidden workload pressure and the trade-offs they are making to stay afloat.

answer: A bit disconnected. I don't really know the team outside of tickets, and I missed the last two socials.
note: They described feeling disconnected and missing the last two socials, which points to lower wellbeing and engagement; the answer did not add clarity or growth signal.

question.label: Support needed
question.name: What kind of support would make the next couple of weeks easier for you?
question.description: Opens up practical support needs without presuming the problem is personal or performance-related.

answer: The design-system PR — I got a ton of rework feedback and I'm not sure if I did something wrong or if that's just how reviews go here.
note: They named the design-system PR and uncertainty about whether the rework feedback reflects their mistake or local review norms, which strongly increases clarity; the answer was more confused than disengaged, so engagement stays flat.

question.label: Review friction
question.name: I wanted to ask about the design-system PR — what made that one hard to land?
question.description: Surfaces the specific source of friction in the rework-heavy PR without assuming fault.

answer: I've been putting off the follow-up on that PR. Every time I open it I feel a bit sick.
note: They described active avoidance of the PR follow-up and a physical sick feeling when opening it, signaling disengagement and a strong wellbeing hit.

question.label: Next step focus
question.name: What do you want to get back to feeling steady on first?
question.description: Gets at the most immediate priority for recovery or re-engagement from their point of view.

answer: Priorities — I'm not sure. I just do what's on my board. I can't really tell what's important vs busywork.
note: They said they can't tell what's important vs busywork, which is a strong clarity signal; the answer did not add new engagement signal beyond the existing drift.

question.label: Ownership shift
question.name: What parts of the frontend work still feel worth leaning into for you?
question.description: Checks whether motivation and ownership are still present in specific areas of the role.

answer: Not really. I'm usually scrolling at 11pm trying to not think about work.
note: The answer shows strong disengagement and avoidance at night, with no sign of stretch or learning interest.

question.label: Feedback texture
question.name: How has the feedback on your recent work landed for you?
question.description: Explores whether feedback is useful, discouraging, or unclear in practice.

answer: I probably don't ask for enough feedback. I don't want to seem like I don't know what I'm doing.
note: He said he avoids asking for feedback because he doesn't want to seem incompetent, which signals some clarity-seeking but no clear growth uplift from the answer itself.

question.label: Stretch gap
question.name: Where do you feel like you're not getting enough stretch right now?
question.description: Surfaces plateau, under-challenge, or a mismatch between current tasks and growth needs.

note: The question was skipped, so no growth or engagement signal was realized.
